title: evax pygmaea subsp. carpetana
intro: Evax pygmaea subsp. carpetana, also known as Rock Evax or Small Evax, is a plant species in the daisy family, native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestion. In herbal preparations, Evax pygmaea subsp. carpetana is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
